## What Was Built

A production-ready CSV parser library in TypeScript with comprehensive test coverage.

   - 40 test cases in JavaScript
   - Can be run with Node.js
   - Identical test coverage

### Documentation Files
5. **README.md**
   - Complete feature documentation
   - API reference
   - Usage examples
   - Implementation highlights

6. **USAGE.md**
   - Quick start guide
   - Common patterns
   - Type reference
   - Troubleshooting

## Features Implemented

### 1. Basic CSV Parsing вњ“
- Parse CSV string to array of objects
- First row treated as headers
- All rows accessible with header names as keys

### 2. Quoted Field Support вњ“
- Handles fields enclosed in quote characters
- Preserves content including commas inside quotes
- Works with custom quote characters

### 3. Escaped Quotes вњ“
- Two consecutive quotes = one literal quote
- Example: `"He said ""Hello"""` в†’ `He said "Hello"`
- Works with custom quote characters

### 4. Custom Delimiters вњ“
- Configurable delimiter (default: comma)
- Configurable quote character (default: double quote)
- Configurable line separator (default: newline)
- All three options independently configurable

### 5. Type Coercion вњ“
- Auto-detects and converts numbers (int and float)
- Converts booleans (true/false, case-insensitive)
- Converts nulls (null, NULL, empty string в†’ null)
- Keeps everything else as strings
- Per-value type detection

### 6. Error Handling вњ“
- Doesn't crash on malformed rows
- Collects errors with row number and description
- Includes error types:
  - Unclosed quotes
  - Field count mismatch
  - Other parsing errors
- Valid rows continue to be processed

### 7. Filtering вњ“
- filter(predicate) method
- Returns matching rows
- Uses standard JavaScript predicate function
- Chainable with other operations

### 8. Aggregation Methods вњ“
- **sum(column)** - Sum numeric values in column
- **average(column)** - Average numeric values
- **count()** - Total number of rows
- **groupBy(column)** - Group rows by column value, returns Map
- All methods skip non-numeric/non-comparable values appropriately

## Return Value Structure

### CSVResult Interface
```typescript
{
  rows: CSVRow[],              // Parsed data rows
  headers: string[],           // Column names
  errors: ParseError[],        // Parsing errors found
  filter: (predicate) => CSVRow[],
  sum: (column: string) => number,
  average: (column: string) => number,
  count: () => number,
  groupBy: (column: string) => Map<CoercedValue, CSVRow[]>
}
```

## Test Coverage

### 40 Test Cases Covering:

**Basic Functionality (4 tests)**
- Test 1: Basic parsing
- Test 2: Quoted fields with commas
- Test 3: Escaped quotes
- Test 4: Custom delimiters

**Type Coercion (6 tests)**
- Test 5: Integer coercion
- Test 6: Float coercion
- Test 7: Boolean coercion
- Test 8: Null value coercion
- Test 9: String preservation
- Test 40: Mixed boolean/string values

**Error Handling (3 tests)**
- Test 10: Mismatched field count
- Test 11: Unclosed quote
- Test 38: Multiple errors

**Filtering (3 tests)**
- Test 12: Basic filtering
- Test 13: String matching
- Test 33: No matches case

**Aggregations (8 tests)**
- Test 14: Sum basic
- Test 15: Sum with mixed types
- Test 16: Average basic
- Test 17: Average with mixed types
- Test 18: Count method
- Test 19: GroupBy basic
- Test 20: GroupBy numeric keys
- Test 21: GroupBy with nulls

**Edge Cases (8 tests)**
- Test 22: Empty input
- Test 23: Single column
- Test 24: Single row
- Test 25: Very large field (10KB)
- Test 34: Average with no numeric values
- Test 35: GroupBy on filtered data
- Test 36: Sum on filtered data
- Test 37: Quoted empty field

**Custom Options (3 tests)**
- Test 26: Custom line separator
- Test 27: Custom quote character
- Test 28: Escaped custom quotes
- Test 29: Tab delimiter
- Test 30: Complex quoted field
- Test 31: Multiple custom options
- Test 32: Special characters in fields
- Test 39: Whitespace preservation

## Code Quality

- **Type Safety**: Full TypeScript interfaces and types
- **No Dependencies**: Zero external libraries
- **Single Pass**: O(n) parsing algorithm
- **Error Resilience**: Malformed data doesn't crash
- **Clean Code**: Well-structured, readable, documented
- **Performance**: Efficient character-by-character parsing

## How to Use

### TypeScript
```typescript
import { parseCSV } from './csv-parser';

const result = parseCSV(csvString, options?);
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const { parseCSV } = require('./csv-parser.js');

const result = parseCSV(csvString, options);
```

## Example

```typescript
const csv = `name,age,active
John,30,true
Jane,25,false
Bob,35,yes`;

const result = parseCSV(csv);

console.log(result.count());           // 3
console.log(result.rows[0].age);       // 30 (number)
console.log(result.rows[1].active);    // false (boolean)
console.log(result.rows[2].active);    // 'yes' (string)

const adults = result.filter(r => r.age >= 30);
const avgAge = result.average('age');  // 30
const grouped = result.groupBy('active');
```

## Summary

вњ“ All 8 features implemented
вњ“ 40 test cases passing
вњ“ Full TypeScript and JavaScript versions
вњ“ Complete documentation
вњ“ Production-ready code
вњ“ Zero dependencies
вњ“ Comprehensive error handling
вњ“ Type-safe interfaces

The library is ready for production use:

2. Copy csv-parser.js to your JavaScript project
3. Import/require and start using parseCSV()
4. Check README.md and USAGE.md for examples

# CSV Parser Library - Complete Package

## рџ“¦ Package Contents

### Core Implementation (2 files)
- **csv-parser.ts** - TypeScript implementation (5.0 KB)
- **csv-parser.js** - JavaScript version (4.1 KB)

### Comprehensive Tests (2 files)
- **csv-parser.test.ts** - TypeScript test suite (17 KB, 40 tests)
- **csv-parser.test.js** - JavaScript test suite (17 KB, 40 tests)

### Documentation (3 files)
- **README.md** - Full documentation with API reference
- **USAGE.md** - Quick start guide and examples
- **DELIVERY.md** - Delivery summary and checklist

### Utilities
- **test-runner.sh** - Validation script
- **INDEX.md** - This file

## рџљЂ Quick Start

### 1. TypeScript Project
```typescript
import { parseCSV } from './csv-parser';

const result = parseCSV(`name,age
John,30
Jane,25`);

console.log(result.rows);    // Array of objects
console.log(result.headers); // ['name', 'age']
```

### 2. JavaScript Project
```javascript
const { parseCSV } = require('./csv-parser.js');

const result = parseCSV(csvString);
```

## вњЁ Features

вњ“ Basic CSV parsing (string в†’ array of objects)
вњ“ Quoted field support (handles commas in fields)
вњ“ Escaped quotes (double quotes = single quote)
вњ“ Custom delimiters (comma, semicolon, tab, pipe, etc.)
вњ“ Type coercion (numbers, booleans, nulls, strings)
вњ“ Error handling (collects errors, continues parsing)
вњ“ Filtering (filter(predicate) method)
вњ“ Aggregation (sum, average, count, groupBy)

## рџ“Љ Test Coverage

- 40 comprehensive test cases
- All features covered
- Edge cases handled
- Error scenarios tested

### Test Categories
- Basic parsing (4 tests)
- Type coercion (6 tests)
- Error handling (3 tests)
- Filtering (3 tests)
- Aggregations (8 tests)
- Edge cases (8 tests)
- Custom options (8 tests)

## рџ”§ API Reference

### Main Function
```typescript
parseCSV(input: string, options?: CSVOptions): CSVResult
```

### Options
```typescript
{
  delimiter?: string,        // Default: ','
  quoteChar?: string,        // Default: '"'
  lineSeparator?: string     // Default: '\n'
}
```

### Result Object
```typescript
{
  rows: CSVRow[],
  headers: string[],
  errors: ParseError[],
  filter: (predicate) => CSVRow[],
  sum: (column: string) => number,
  average: (column: string) => number,
  count: () => number,
  groupBy: (column: string) => Map<any, CSVRow[]>
}
```

## рџ’Ў Common Usage Patterns

### Parse and Count
```typescript
const result = parseCSV(csv);
console.log(result.count()); // Total rows
```

### Filter Data
```typescript
const adults = result.filter(row => row.age >= 18);
```

### Sum Column
```typescript
const total = result.sum('amount');
```

### Average Column
```typescript
const avg = result.average('score');
```

### Group By Column
```typescript
const groups = result.groupBy('category');
for (const [category, items] of groups) {
  console.log(category, items.length);
}
```

### Handle Errors
```typescript
if (result.errors.length > 0) {
  result.errors.forEach(err => {
    console.log(`Row ${err.row}: ${err.message}`);
  });
}
```

## рџЋЇ Type Conversions

| Input | Output Type | Example |
|-------|-------------|---------|
| `"123"` | number | `123` |
| `"3.14"` | number | `3.14` |
| `"true"` | boolean | `true` |
| `"false"` | boolean | `false` |
| `""` | null | `null` |
| `"null"` | null | `null` |
| `"text"` | string | `"text"` |

## рџ“‹ Supported CSV Formats

### Standard CSV
```
name,age,city
John,30,New York
```

### Quoted Fields
```
name,address
John,"123 Main St, Apt 4"
```

### Escaped Quotes
```
comment
"He said ""Hello"""
```

### Custom Delimiters
```
// Semicolon-delimited
name;age;city
John;30;New York

// Tab-delimited
name	age	city

================================================================================

  parseCSV(input: string, options?: CSVOptions): CSVResult

Options Object:

  }

Result Object:

    errors: ParseError[],                              // Errors found
    filter: (predicate) => CSVRow[],                   // Filter rows
    sum: (column) => number,                           // Sum column
    average: (column) => number,                       // Average column
    count: () => number,                               // Count rows
    groupBy: (column) => Map<any, CSVRow[]>           // Group by column
  }

Error Object:
  {

  }

```typescript
const result = parseCSV(csvString);
result.rows.forEach(row => console.log(row));

### 2. Filter Data

const adults = result.filter(row => row.age >= 18);

### 3. Sum a Column

### 4. Group Data
```typescript

```

## API Cheat Sheet

```typescript
// Parse CSV
const result = parseCSV(csvString, options);

// Main result object
result.rows      // Array of parsed objects
result.headers   // Column names
result.errors    // Array of parsing errors

// Methods
result.filter(predicate)     // Filter rows
result.sum(columnName)       // Sum numeric values
result.average(columnName)   // Average numeric values
result.count()               // Total rows
result.groupBy(columnName)   // Group by column

// Options (all optional)
{
  delimiter: ',',            // Field separator (default: ',')
  quoteChar: '"',            // Quote character (default: '"')
  lineSeparator: '\n'        // Line separator (default: '\n')
}
```

- Handles fields enclosed in quote characters
- Preserves content including commas inside quotes
- Works with custom quote characters

### 3. Escaped Quotes вњ“
- Two consecutive quotes = one literal quote
- Example: `"He said ""Hello"""` в†’ `He said "Hello"`
- Works with custom quote characters

### 4. Custom Delimiters вњ“
- Configurable delimiter (default: comma)
- Configurable quote character (default: double quote)
- Configurable line separator (default: newline)
- All three options independently configurable

### 5. Type Coercion вњ“
- Auto-detects and converts numbers (int and float)
- Converts booleans (true/false, case-insensitive)
- Converts nulls (null, NULL, empty string в†’ null)
- Keeps everything else as strings
- Per-value type detection

### 6. Error Handling вњ“
- Doesn't crash on malformed rows
- Collects errors with row number and description
- Includes error types:
  - Unclosed quotes
  - Field count mismatch
  - Other parsing errors
- Valid rows continue to be processed

### 7. Filtering вњ“
- filter(predicate) method
- Returns matching rows
- Uses standard JavaScript predicate function
- Chainable with other operations

### 8. Aggregation Methods вњ“
- **sum(column)** - Sum numeric values in column
- **average(column)** - Average numeric values
- **count()** - Total number of rows
- **groupBy(column)** - Group rows by column value, returns Map
- All methods skip non-numeric/non-comparable values appropriately

  rows: CSVRow[],              // Parsed data rows
  headers: string[],           // Column names
